name: CJC-1295 with DAC
subtitle: Long-Acting Growth Hormone Releasing Hormone Analog | Extended Release

modifications: Enables weekly dosing vs pulsatile release

overview: Modified growth hormone releasing hormone engineered for extended duration via albumin-binding technology. DAC binds to albumin, extending half-life and providing continuous GHRH receptor stimulation.
mechanism: DAC binds to albumin, extending half-life to 6-8 days and providing continuous GHRH receptor stimulation for sustained GH/IGF-1 elevation.

items.name: Sustained GH Elevation
items.description: Continuous growth hormone release for 6-8 days per injection.

items.name: IGF-1 Optimization
items.description: Significantly elevates IGF-1 levels for extended periods.

items.name: Convenience Protocol
items.description: Weekly dosing ideal for simple administration.

items.name: Fat Loss Enhancement
items.description: Continuous GH elevation promotes lipolysis and fat metabolism.

items.name: Lean Mass Gains
items.description: Sustained anabolic environment supports muscle protein synthesis.

items.name: Recovery Enhancement
items.description: Accelerated healing and recovery from exercise or injury.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the preferred route for DAC variant.

steps: Clean both vial tops with alcohol and dry completely
Inject water slowly to prevent foaming
Let sit 5 minutes before gently rolling to mix
Solution may appear slightly cloudy initially
Label with date and concentration immediately

interactions.notes: Continuous GH elevation may reduce synergistic benefits of pulsatile protocols.
GHRP pulse effects diminished with continuous GH elevation.
Never combine different variants; choose based on desired release pattern.
Both provide continuous GH elevation; combination risks excessive levels.
Both are GHRH analogs; no additional benefit and potential receptor competition.
Defeats purpose and may suppress natural production.
CJC-1295 DAC is already more potent and long-lasting.

timeline.effects: Possible water retention, improved sleep, increased appetite
Noticeable recovery improvements, some report joint discomfort
Visible body composition changes, increased vascularity
Continued improvements but watch for desensitization signs
Most gains maintained; IGF-1 returns to baseline in 2-3 weeks

stopSigns: Severe joint pain or carpal tunnel syndrome
Excessive water retention affecting daily life
Signs of acromegaly (jaw growth, hand/feet enlargement)
monitoring: Regular IGF-1 monitoring recommended for long-term use
Higher incidence of side effects than pulsatile protocols

good: High purity requirement (>98%); impurities cause more side effects
Proper DAC labeling; legitimate products clearly state 'with DAC'
warning: Higher cost than non-DAC (2-3x more due to complex manufacturing)
Foaming during mixing is normal for DAC peptides; wait for foam to settle
bad: Extremely cheap pricing (if priced similar to non-DAC, likely mislabeled)
Clear solution immediately (should be slightly cloudy initially)

references.title: Prolonged Growth Hormone Elevation with CJC-1295 DAC
references.keyFindings: 12 subjects; sustained GH and IGF-1 elevation for 6-10 days post-injection with peak 2-4x baseline.

references.title: Dose-Response Relationship of CJC-1295 DAC
references.keyFindings: 48 subjects, 90 days. Optimal dosing 1-2mg weekly; dose-dependent IGF-1 increases but higher side effects above 2mg.

references.title: Long-term Effects on GH/IGF-1 Axis
references.keyFindings: 6-month animal model. Sustained IGF-1 elevation but evidence of reduced GH pulse amplitude suggesting desensitization.

references.title: Comparative Pharmacokinetics: DAC vs Non-DAC Variants
references.keyFindings: DAC: 120-hour half-life vs non-DAC: 30-minute; non-DAC preserves more physiological patterns.

quickStats.frequency: Once or twice weekly (e.g., Monday/Thursday for split dosing)
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: 2-8°C refrigerated; Reconstituted: 2-8°C refrigerated, use within 30 days
